title: Facilitating direct rider driver pairing for mass egress areas
abstract: A transport facilitation system can identify a mass egress area within a given region by transmitting unique match codes to rider devices. A rider application executing on the rider device can initiate a late-binding state facilitating a direct pairing between the rider and driver.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A transport facilitation system comprising : one or more processors; and one or more memory resources storing instructions that, when executed by the one or more processors, cause the one or more processors to: identify a mass egress area within a given region; receive a pick-up request from a rider device within the mass egress area; in response to receiving the pick-up request, transmit a match code to the rider device; receive an indication of the match code from a driver device, the indication signifying a pick-up event between a requesting user of the rider device and an available driver of the driver device within the mass egress area; and record a state for both the rider device and the driver device indicating an initiation of a ride to a destination of the requesting user. 2. The transport facilitation system of claim 1, wherein the executed instructions further cause the one or more processors to: provide a rider application for execution on rider devices and a driver application for execution on driver devices, each of the rider application and driver application being specific to an on-demand transportation service managed by the transport facilitation system for the given region; wherein the pick-up request is received from the rider device via the executing rider application on the rider device, and wherein the indication of the match code is received via the executing driver application on the driver device. 3. The transport facilitation system of claim 2, wherein the executed instructions further cause the one or more processors to: based on identifying the mass egress area, generate a geo-fence area including at least a portion of the mass egress area, wherein the driver 

 device, executing the driver application, operates in a normal service state when the available driver is external to the geo-fence area. 4. The transport facilitation system of claim 3, wherein the executed instructions further cause the one or more processors to: receive, via a location-based resource on the driver device, a dynamic current location of the available driver traveling throughout the given region; based on the dynamic current location of the available driver, detect the driver device entering the geo-fence area; and in response to the driver device entering the geo-fence area, initiate, via the executing driver application on the driver device, a late-binding state on the driver device, the late-binding state to enabling reception of the match code on the driver device to pair the available driver with the requesting user. 5. The transport facilitation system of claim 1, wherein the match code comprises a personal identification number (PIN) to be displayed on a display screen of the rider device, and wherein receiving the indication of the match code from the driver device corresponds to the available driver inputting the PIN on the driver device. 6. The transport facilitation system of claim 1, wherein the match code comprises a quick response (QR) code to be displayed on a display screen of the rider device, and wherein receiving the indication of the match code from the driver device corresponds to the available driver scanning the QR code with the driver device. 7. The transport facilitation system of claim 1, wherein the match code comprises an ultrasonic signature to be outputted by an audio system of the rider device, and wherein receiving the indication of the match code from the driver device corresponds to the driver device detecting the ultrasonic signature from the rider device. 8. A mobile computing device comprising : a display screen; 

 one or more processors; and one or more memory resources storing instructions that, when executed by the one or more processors, cause the one or more processors to: in response to a user input by a user of the mobile computing device, initiate a rider application for an on-demand transportation service; based on the mobile computing device being in a mass egress area, initiate a late-binding state on the rider application to enable a direct pairing between the user and an available driver; in the late-binding state, receive a unique match code from a backend transport system that manages the on-demand transportation service; and display the unique match code on the display screen to facilitate the direct pairing. 9. The mobile computing device of claim 8, wherein displaying the unique match code on the display screen enables the user to present the unique match to the available driver for entry on a driver device, and wherein entry of the unique match code causes the driver device to transmit data to the backend transport system indicating the direct pairing between the user and the available driver. 10. The mobile computing device of claim 9, wherein entry of the unique match code on the driver device triggers an on-trip status by the backend transport system for both the mobile computing device and the driver device. 11. The mobile computing device of claim 8, further comprising : a location-based resource to detect a current location of the mobile computing device; wherein initiation of the rider application causes the one or more processors to: (i) detect the current location of the mobile computing device using the location-based resource; and (ii) based on detecting that the mobile computing device is within the mass egress area, generate a late- 

 binding notification screen on the display screen, the late-binding notification screen indicating that the late-binding state is initiated. 12. The mobile computing device of claim 8, wherein a user interface of the rider application includes a state switching feature that enables the user to switch from a normal state to the late-binding state of the rider application. 13. The mobile computing device of claim 12, wherein the normal state of the rider application enables the user to transmit a pick-up request to the backend transport system, causing the backend transport system to select a driver to rendezvous with the user at a given pick-up location. 14. The mobile computing device of claim 12, wherein the executed instructions further cause the one or more processors to: in the normal state of the rider application, receive a user input on the state switching feature; wherein the executed instructions cause the one or more processors to initiate the late-binding state to receive the unique match code in response to the user input on the state switching feature. 15. A non-transitory computer readable medium storing instructions that, when executed by one or more processors of a mobile computing device, cause the one or more processors to: in response to a user input by a user of the mobile computing device, initiate a rider application for an on-demand transportation service; based on the mobile computing device being in a mass egress area, initiate a late-binding state on the rider application to enable a direct pairing between the user and an available driver; in the late-binding state, receive a unique match code from a backend transport system that manages the on-demand transportation service; and display the unique match code on the display screen of the mobile computing device to facilitate the direct pairing. 16. The non-transitory computer readable medium of claim 15, wherein displaying the unique match code on the display screen enables the user to present the unique match to the available driver for entry on a driver device, and wherein entry of the unique match code causes the driver device to transmit data to the backend transport system indicating the direct pairing between the user and the available driver. 17. The non-transitory computer readable medium of claim 16, wherein entry of the unique match code on the driver device triggers an on- trip status by the backend transport system for both the mobile computing device and the driver device. 18. The non-transitory computer readable medium of claim 15, wherein the mobile computing device includes a location-based resource to detect a current location of the mobile computing device, and wherein initiation of the rider application causes the one or more processors to: (i) detect the current location of the mobile computing device using the location- based resource; and (ii) based on detecting that the mobile computing device is within the mass egress area, generate a late-binding notification screen on the display screen, the late-binding notification screen indicating that the late-binding state is initiated. 19. The non-transitory computer readable medium of claim 15, wherein a user interface of the rider application includes a state switching feature that enables the user to switch from a normal state to the late- binding state of the rider application. 20. The non-transitory computer readable medium of claim 19, wherein the normal state of the rider application enables the user to transmit a pick-up request to the backend transport system, causing the backend transport system to select a driver to rendezvous with the user at a given pick-up location.
